deviations.description: The process model enforces that both physical examination and blood test are performed before making a diagnosis, as shown in the process flow on page 1. Version 2 of the requirement permits making a diagnosis even if these steps have not been performed. The process unnecessarily restricts behavior by requiring these steps, exceeding the new, more permissive requirement.
process_model_reference: Perform Physical Examination, Draw Blood Samples, Blood Analysis Requested, Make Diagnosis

deviations.description: The process model enforces that a blood test must precede diagnosis. Version 2 removes this obligation, but the process still requires it, resulting in unnecessary restriction.
process_model_reference: Draw Blood Samples, Blood Analysis Requested, Make Diagnosis

deviations.description: The modality change from obligation to permission means the process is allowed, but not required, to make a diagnosis without prior physical examination or blood test. The process model still enforces the stricter, outdated obligation.

The compliance pattern change from 'A_precedes_B' to 'existence_of_A' removes the requirement for a specific order or even the necessity of the preceding activities. The process model still enforces the outdated order, resulting in over-compliance.
process_model_reference: Perform Physical Examination, Draw Blood Samples, Make Diagnosis

deviations.description: The process model still requires both physical examination and blood test before diagnosis, while the new requirement only refers to the existence of a diagnosis, regardless of prior activities.
process_model_reference: Perform Physical Examination, Draw Blood Samples, Make Diagnosis

deviations.description: The process model continues to enforce the deleted obligation that a blood test must precede diagnosis, which is no longer required in Version 2.
process_model_reference: Draw Blood Samples, Blood Analysis Requested, Make Diagnosis
